title: Version/CHANGELOG mismatch: unreleased user-facing changes accumulate under already-closed 1.2.1 heading; needs a version bump before release
evidence: pyproject version=1.2.1; __version__=1.2.1; CHANGELOG has closed '[1.2.1] - 2026-07-17' AND a populated [Unreleased] with new Changed/Fixed items; PyPI latest=1.2.0; v1.2.1 NOT tagged
impact: Releasing now would ship a version number whose changelog entry is already dated/closed while real changes sit under [Unreleased]; consumers cannot tell what shipped
recommended_action: Decide the next version (recommend 1.3.0: new behavior + features since 1.2.1 was cut) OR fold [Unreleased] into a fresh version heading; bump pyproject + __version__ to match; this is a release-notes decision, deferred to the user's Go
source_files: twine check PASS; version strings consistent at 1.2.1 currently
validation: Present as the central release decision in Go/No-Go; do not auto-bump

title: CI matrix tops out at Python 3.13 while requires-python>=3.9 and local dev is 3.14
evidence: .github/workflows/test.yml matrix 3.9-3.13; local venv 3.14; suite passes on 3.14 locally
impact: 3.14 is not exercised in CI; a 3.14-only regression could slip
recommended_action: Optionally add 3.14 to the matrix when GitHub runners support it reliably; low priority

title: Pre-existing static type-hint noise in src/vistab.py and tests (Optional/bool annotations vs runtime idioms like header=False)
evidence: LSP reports ~90 type errors, all pre-existing annotation mismatches (e.g. header: Optional[Iterable] but header=False is a supported idiom); no runtime impact
impact: Cosmetic; type-checker noise; not a correctness defect
recommended_action: Deferred: a full type-annotation cleanup is a Medium-complexity refactor touching many public signatures with risk of changing the typed public contract; out of scope for this release
validation: Defer (Complexity axis); could be a future typing pass
